P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Technical Assistance 
(916) 322-5662 
Jame s D. Palme r 
Administration •• 
Executive/legal 
322·5660 
322·5901 
July 29 1983 
Enforcement 
322-64.41 
Statements of Economic Interest 
322-6444 
Office of the District Attorney 
Courthouse, 4th Floor 
P.O. Box 670 
Martinez, CA 
94553 
Dear Mr. Palmer: 
Re: 
Your Request for Written 
Advice, Our Advice 
No. A-83-182 
You have asked us for written advice to clarify your 
reporting responsibilities as to your investments. 
As I 
understand the facts, your disclosure category requires that you 
report all investmentsl/ in and income from persons situated 
or doing business in your jurisdiction. 
Persons is defined to 
include businesses. 2/ 
You must also report all interests in 
real property 3/ situated in the jurisdiction or within two 
miles of the jurisdiction. 4/ 
You own a duplex in Sacramento, 
rented to tenants who live and work in Sacramento, financed by a 
loan from Wells Fargo Mortgage Co. out of Santa Rosa. 
You own a 
4.5% interest in a house in Contra Costa County (your juris-
diction) 
You also hold stocks in numerous companies which 
1/ 
The Political Reform Act (Government Code Sections 
81000-91014) define "investments" to include stocks, bonds, 
options, etc. 
Government Code Section 82034. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
specified. 
~/ Section 82047. 
3/ Section 82033. 
if Section 82035. 


James D. Pa lrner 
July 29, 1983 
Page 2 
are publicly traded on the major stock exchanges. 
Your wife is 
employed by a trucking company situated in Alameda County, but 
which does some business in Contra Costa County. 
You wish to be 
sure of your reporting obligations as to the foregoing. 
You need not report your interest in the duplex in 
Sacramento since this real property is not situated in, nor 
within two miles of, your jurisdiction. 
You must report your 
real property interest in the house in Contra Costa County if 
your interest is worth more than $1,000 and if it is not your 
personal residence (I understand that it is not) • 
You need not report the income from the rental of your 
duplex in Sacramento since it and the tenants are located 
outside the jurisdiction and they are: 
••• not doing business within the jurisdiction, not 
planning to do business within the jurisdiction, or not 
having done business within the jurisdiction during the 
two years prior to the time any statement or other 
action is required under this title. 
Section 82030(a) • 
However, since a loan is considered income (Section 
82030(a», you must report the outstanding balance of the loan 
from Wells Fargo Mortgage if the balance exceeded $10,00051 at 
any time during the reporting period since Wells Fargo is 
assuredly "doing business" in Contra Costa County even though 
the loan is through Santa Rosa. 
You must also report any income 
received from the house in Contra Costa County if it equals $250 
or more. 
You must also list your community property share of 
income from your wife's employer if your share equals $250 or 
more. 
As for your investments, you must report investments (direct 
or indirect)&! which have had a value of more than $1,000 at 
any time during the reporting period if the business entity in 
which the investment is held or any parent or subsidiary or 
otherwise related business entity: 
51 
Section 82030 (b) (8) • 
il See Section 82034. 


Jame s D. Palmer 
July 29, 1983 
Page 3 
••• has an interest in real property in the 
jurisdiction, or does business or plans to do business 
in the jurisdiction, or has done business in the 
jurisdiction at any time during the two years prior to 
the time any statement ••• is required under this 
title. 
Section 82034. 
You have specifically asked regarding clarification of what 
constitutes "doing business" within the jurisdiction. I enclose 
for your review the Commission's Opinion requested by David R. 
Baty, No. 77-011, May 1, 1979, 5 FPPC Opinions 10, which 
discusses this topic in some detail. 
In short, the Baty Opinion 
states: 
Although none of the corporations is headquartered in 
California, each has either distribution or 
manufacturing facilities in California and the products 
manufactured by each are sold on a regular basis 
throughout the state. 
Any of these activities engaged 
in by the corporations standing alone would be 
sufficient to create a potential for conflicts of 
interest on the part of an official having an 
investment in the corporations. 
(Emphasis added.) 
5 FPPC Opinions at 12. 
I trust that this letter has clarified these matters for 
you. 
However, should you have further questions, please do not 
hesitate to contact me at 916/322-5901.

Sincerely,

sections.question: You have asked us for written advice to clarify your reporting responsibilities as to your investments. As I understand the facts, your disclosure category requires that you report all investments in and income from persons situated or doing business in your jurisdiction. Persons is defined to include businesses. You must also report all interests in real property situated in the jurisdiction or within two miles of the jurisdiction. You own a duplex in Sacramento, rented to tenants who live and work in Sacramento, financed by a loan from Wells Fargo Mortgage Co. out of Santa Rosa. You own a 4.5% interest in a house in Contra Costa County (your jurisdiction). You also hold stocks in numerous companies which are publicly traded on the major stock exchanges. Your wife is employed by a trucking company situated in Alameda County, but which does some business in Contra Costa County. You wish to be sure of your reporting obligations as to the foregoing.
sections.conclusion: You need not report your interest in the duplex in Sacramento since this real property is not situated in, nor within two miles of, your jurisdiction. You must report your real property interest in the house in Contra Costa County if your interest is worth more than $1,000 and if it is not your personal residence. You need not report the income from the rental of your duplex in Sacramento since it and the tenants are located outside the jurisdiction. However, you must report the outstanding balance of the loan from Wells Fargo Mortgage if the balance exceeded $10,000 at any time during the reporting period. You must also report any income received from the house in Contra Costa County if it equals $250 or more and list your community property share of income from your wife's employer if your share equals $250 or more. You must report investments which have had a value of more than $1,000 at any time during the reporting period if the business entity has an interest in real property in the jurisdiction, or does business or plans to do business in the jurisdiction.
sections.question_synthetic: A District Attorney inquires about his reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act regarding investments, real property interests, and income from various sources including a duplex, a house interest, stocks, and his wife's employment.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that the requestor must report the Contra Costa County house interest (if worth over $1,000 and not a personal residence), the Wells Fargo loan balance (if exceeding $10,000), income from the Contra Costa property and wife's employer (if $250 or more each), and investments in companies doing or planning business in the jurisdiction (if valued over $1,000), but need not report the Sacramento duplex interest or its rental income.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a formal FPPC advice letter dated July 29, 1983. Question and conclusion sections were reconstructed from narrative paragraphs as no explicit section headers were present. Clear statutory citations provided throughout. Includes reference to prior FPPC opinion (77-011) for clarification on 'doing business' definition.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You have asked us for written advice to clarify your reporting responsibilities as to your investments. As I understand the facts, your disclosure category requires that you report all investments in and income from persons situated or doing business in your jurisdiction. Persons is defined to include businesses. You must also report all interests in real property situated in the jurisdiction or within two miles of the jurisdiction. You own a duplex in Sacramento, rented to tenants who live and work in Sacramento, financed by a loan from Wells Fargo Mortgage Co. out of Santa Rosa. You own a 4.5% interest in a house in Contra Costa County (your jurisdiction). You also hold stocks in numerous companies which are publicly traded on the major stock exchanges. Your wife is employed by a trucking company situated in Alameda County, but which does some business in Contra Costa County. You wish to be sure of your reporting obligations as to the foregoing.

CONCLUSION: You need not report your interest in the duplex in Sacramento since this real property is not situated in, nor within two miles of, your jurisdiction. You must report your real property interest in the house in Contra Costa County if your interest is worth more than $1,000 and if it is not your personal residence. You need not report the income from the rental of your duplex in Sacramento since it and the tenants are located outside the jurisdiction. However, you must report the outstanding balance of the loan from Wells Fargo Mortgage if the balance exceeded $10,000 at any time during the reporting period. You must also report any income received from the house in Contra Costa County if it equals $250 or more and list your community property share of income from your wife's employer if your share equals $250 or more. You must report investments which have had a value of more than $1,000 at any time during the reporting period if the business entity has an interest in real property in the jurisdiction, or does business or plans to do business in the jurisdiction.

embedding.summary: The FPPC provides detailed guidance to a District Attorney regarding his reporting obligations under the Political Reform Act for various investments and real property interests, clarifying which assets must be reported based on their location, value thresholds, and connection to his jurisdiction.
